note: Historical iteration narrative (v1.6–v10.x) archived at storage/_archive/auto_improve_memory_pre_v69.json when v6.9 was re-crowned on 2026-04-14. Only anti-patterns + hard patterns are carried forward here.
known_hard_patterns: Sites using Wix/Shopify/Squarespace — heavy JS, minimal server HTML
ATS platforms with company slugs in URL path
Sites where job listings are in modals
Pagination via infinite scroll / Load More buttons
Sites with no CSS classes at all — layout via inline styles only
Discovery targets returning non-HTML payloads (PDF/download) even on career-related hosts
ATS hosts where the valid listing path is nested and locale-specific (e.g. /CandidatePortal/en-AU/<slug>/ or /hcmUI/CandidateExperience/.../requisitions)
Discovery that lands on a single job detail URL while list pages exist on the same host
ATS tenants that redirect from tenant subdomains to global login hosts; probing must still include the original tenant host
Job pages where parent extraction times out due heavy detail enrichment/network fetches before local DOM fallbacks can run
JS app shells exposing only config data unless deeper listing paths are discovered (e.g. /jobs/search or /embed-jobs)
Job-board state payloads that mix real jobs with department/filter/company metadata in the same JSON tree
Next.js boards with script-heavy bodies and empty app roots where raw text length looks high but visible DOM listings are absent
Career pages that mix editorial campaign headings with real vacancy headings in the same prose/accordion block
RSS/XML job feeds can include long historical archives and require feed-specific caps/validation to avoid over-capture
State-script pages where visible DOM has no listings (only __NEXT_DATA__/client metadata) require JSON parsing or API probes to avoid false negatives
Oracle CandidateExperience tenants may expose multiple site IDs (CX, CX_1001, etc.); wrong tenant path silently under-captures jobs
Localized WordPress job pages can use generic URLs/title nouns with repeated Elementor cards and 'Info Lengkap' CTAs, which are easy to miss from homepage-only discovery
Next.js dynamic boards where server HTML is empty and listings are only exposed through _next/data JSON endpoints
Boilerplate phrase filtering that uses raw substring matching can collide with valid titles (e.g., 'deSIGN INtern') and silently under-capture jobs
Multi-candidate extraction can still under-capture when scoring prefers high-confidence subsets over larger overlapping sets
Title/URL substring collisions in validation rules (e.g., '/account' in '/accountant', 'sign in' inside 'design intern') can silently drop valid jobs
Document-first career pages where vacancies are published as PDF/Doc links and job titles are embedded in link text
Oracle CandidateExperience APIs that return requisitions nested under items[].requisitionList instead of flat item rows
Oracle CandidateExperience hosts that expose multiple tenant site IDs can look equally valid in HTML shells; discovery tie-breaks may lock onto generic CX paths unless tenant-sp...
what_works_well: Post-extraction exact-title filtering for pagination/section labels (for example `Show N more`, `Working with us`) removes Type-1 noise with minimal volume risk when paired with URL checks like `/show_more`.
Role-gated Elementor card extraction with link evidence recovers multilingual lowongan-style job grids while rejecting section headings.
Hidden-input (`job_title_*`) harvesting plus accordion/button context recovers card-based same-page ATS listings without requiring detail URLs.
Host-diversified Martian probing (tenant + web.martianlogic + myrecruitmentplus host families) improves recovery from config-only Next.js shells.
ApplyFlow config-driven endpoint probing (`afConfig` + seeker/wp-json/af-api variants) recovers jobs when static HTML lacks `.af-search-result-item` rows.
Retrying hint URLs with TLS-relaxed fallback and scheme alternates can recover real listing pages on certificate-mismatch domains without broad discovery churn.
Linked-card extraction that reads inner title nodes (not whole anchor text) plus bounded same-host pagination improves card-board coverage while suppressing nav-label false posi...
ATS-first Jobvite table extraction (`.jv-job-list` row parsing with /job/ detail URL gating) prevents seeker-tool CTA false positives and improves multilingual title coverage.
Parsing quoted Jobs2Web `ssoCompanyId`/`ssoUrl` config and probing sso-host career/search endpoints improves recovery on branded SAP shells with empty server-rendered job DOM.
Including `div.pagination`/`nav-links` containers in bounded same-host pagination follow-up improves page-2+ coverage on WordPress-style listing pages.
Ranking Jobs2Web probes to try same-host search URLs early (while retaining API jobsearch probes) improves recovery on config-shell SAP pages within fixed request budgets.
Platform-aware careers-page API recovery (baseUrl/clientSlug -> `/api/v1.0/c/<slug>/jobs`) improves volume and metadata depth over SSR-only card extraction on Manatal portals.
Connx post-extraction URL repair that backfills `/job/details/...` links from inline row evidence improves quality matching when heuristic extraction returns same-page listing U...
Rejecting date-only/listing-filter labels in title validation prevents linked-card metadata/date artifacts from being emitted as jobs.
Treating `/jobs?...` filter/query URLs as non-detail targets reduces job-board taxonomy/filter false positives without weakening true detail-ID query handling.
Supplementing linked-card extraction with bounded `/jobs/<id>/...` numeric-detail fallback recovers legacy high-volume vacancy tables that fail strict noun-only title gates.
Role-grid extraction that preserves same-title/same-URL rows when location differs recovers legitimate multi-opening boards without broad title-validation relaxation.
Open-state accordion extraction (`data-state="open"` + heading + detail link + labeled location) recovers modern CMS/Radix career modules with low Type-1 risk.
Jobs2Web shell probing that includes parsed `ssoUrl` host variants and bounded `/services/search/*` fallbacks improves recovery when standard API/referrer endpoints are sparse.
Preserving trusted same-page anchor vacancies (Elementor/open-state) through post-filtering/dedupe keeps valid employment-page jobs that would otherwise be dropped by broad non-...
Dedicated careers-page `.job-card` extraction with bounded `?page=` follow-up recovers high-volume ATS boards more reliably than generic linked-card fallback.
what_doesnt_work: Leaving listing pagination anchors (`/show_more`, `Show N more`) in final jobsets leaks obvious non-job titles and degrades precision.
Site-specific hacks (Teamtailor-specific, SuccessFactors-specific, etc.) — don't generalise
Deep inheritance chains (>3 levels) — become fragile and break parent logic
Overriding extract() without calling super() — breaks the tier cascade
Adding 5+ methods per version — too much change, can't isolate what breaks
Path probing with synthetic URL repairs (/career to /careers-1) — false positives
Accepting tiny candidate sets (<=3) without strong title/url evidence — causes menu/account false positives
Treating RSS/download links as career candidates — leads to discovery misses
Small-set auto-accept rules (e.g. <=3 rows) without title/url validation — causes menu/footer false positives
Over-strict small-set validation requiring job-like detail URLs for every row — drops valid heading/accordion listings with same-page links
Assuming redirected host is the correct probe target for ATS domains — often loses tenant-specific listing paths
Accepting category/taxonomy titles (e.g., 'All Jobs', '<Department> Jobs') as job postings in link-heavy navigation pages
Using job-board page-level URL hints alone as evidence for JSON nodes with generic 'name' fields
SPA detection that relies only on body-text length misses script-heavy shells with empty rendered roots
Over-prioritizing query-heavy probe URLs (e.g. embed-jobs with paging params) during discovery can outrank cleaner listing paths
Treating generic headings like 'Open Roles', 'Job Description', or corporate section labels as job titles
Accepting single-item jobsets based only on long surrounding text without URL/apply evidence
Using finder link text extraction paths that assume HtmlElement-only APIs (`text_content`) without etree-safe fallback
Trusting generic Oracle /sites/CX paths without checking tenant-specific siteNumber hints can miss most requisitions
Relying on homepage-level career keywords alone on recruiter agency sites increases section/page false positives
Accepting generic CTA labels (e.g., 'Current Jobs', 'Search Jobs', 'Browse Jobs') as job titles causes false positives on careers landing pages
Using fixed Jobs2Web endpoint order that spends the bounded probe budget before same-host `/search` variants are attempted can cause avoidable 0-job misses on SAP shells.
Assuming __NEXT_DATA__ always contains full job objects; many boards require explicit _next/data endpoint fetches
Using unconstrained substring boilerplate checks (instead of phrase boundaries) in title validation causes hard-to-notice false negatives
Pure score-max selection across candidates can choose smaller overlapping sets and lose real jobs even when larger validated sets exist
Guarding global job-link extraction behind 'no other candidates' misses real jobs on pages where parent output is only a partial subset
Short local-text windows for heading rows can miss apply evidence in long cards and under-capture same-page listings
Treating generic /careers or /join-our-team landing URLs as job-detail evidence for non-role titles introduces navigation false positives
Globally rejecting document URLs drops legitimate vacancy postings on organisations that publish role descriptions as PDF links
Assuming Oracle requisition APIs always return flat top-level items misses tenants that nest jobs under requisitionList
Stopping Oracle candidate selection at the first generic CX shell match can suppress richer tenant-specific requisitions paths with similar superficial scores
Relying on GET-only Martian/MyRecruitmentPlus probes misses boards where listing APIs expect POST search payloads
Validating same-page jobsets using only apply phrases in cleaned descriptions drops legitimate vacancies where 'How to apply' text is trimmed
Treating broad /career/* links as equally strong job evidence allows non-listing career navigation pages to outrank true opening/detail links.
Keeping multiple title variants for the same detail URL inflates job counts and degrades title quality on row-group extraction.
Assuming comma-separated title tails are locations by default can degrade title quality on specialization-heavy role names.
Relying only on HTML-visible endpoint hints misses app-shell APIs defined exclusively inside external JavaScript bundles.
Using fixed top-N Martian probes from a single sorted list can exhaust request budget on one host family and miss live APIs on alternate ATS hosts.
Scanning only the first one or two script chunks for endpoint hints under-captures app-shell boards where APIs are declared in page-specific bundles.
Accepting small 1-2 item sets without explicit role and job-evidence checks allows culture/leadership cards to pass as jobs.
Treating listing CTA labels (e.g. Job Openings / Join Our Team) as reject-only text in subpage traversal blocks true listing endpoints.
